title: vepris tabouensis
intro: Vepris tabouensis, also known as the Camphor tree, is a species of plant in the Rutaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant's leaves and bark are rich in camphor oil, which has medicinal properties that include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic effects. The health benefits of Vepris tabouensis include relief from fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or salve, often in combination with other herbs, to maximize its therapeutic effects.
